note: Daily scrape for 2026-05-18 (Monday). 5 pr-marketing leads (funding/launch triggers from past 4 days), 1 fund-formation lead (new VC fund launch), 2 legal-freelance leads (remote fractional GC postings). All deduplicated against daily-scrape, pr-freelance-scrape, and legal-freelance-scrape reports back through 2026-05-12. Signal refresh on 4 prior hot leads (Greenboard, Adfin, Embat, Cardinal/Haven Tower). pr-marketing and pr-freelance routing applied per Mark's rule: business-event leads → pr-marketing; PR/comms job postings → pr-freelance.

leads.name: Fasset
leads.industry: Fintech — stablecoin-powered neobank for emerging markets (multi-currency accounts, debit cards, cross-border payments, stablecoin custody, investment services)
leads.location: Los Angeles, California, USA (operations across MENA, Asia, Africa)
leads.fundingStage: Series B — $51M closed 2026-05-14, co-led by SBI Group (Japan) and Investcorp (Bahrain) with participation from Arz Portföy and strategic family offices; largest Series B in payments sector globally for 2026, top-10 fintech round of 2026
leads.source: fintech.global / CoinDesk / FintechFutures — Fasset Series B coverage
leads.notes: Top new lead 5/18 (combined 80, A). $51M Series B closed 5/14 — largest payments-sector Series B globally for 2026 and a top-10 fintech round of the year. Strong fit: FS/Tech industry, growth-stage, B2B infrastructure layer alongside consumer-facing app, named institutional investors (SBI, Investcorp). Triple trigger: large round, planned headcount tripling, three-continent geographic expansion. CAVEAT: LA-headquartered but operations heavily MENA/Asia/Africa — US-based agency has clearest wedge on US/Americas expansion narrative, English-language financial press, and US investor relations. Confirmed leadership: CEO Mohammad Raafi Hossain. Wedge: 90-day comms sprint around Americas launch + earned media around stablecoin neobank category.

leads.name: GridCARE
leads.industry: AI for energy infrastructure — physics-based AI platform (Energize™) that identifies and activates near-term grid capacity for AI factories, utilities, and energy providers
leads.fundingStage: Series A — $64M oversubscribed, closed 2026-05-14, led by Sutter Hill Ventures and John Doerr; participation from National Grid Partners, Future Energy Ventures, Emerson Collective, Stanford University; significant valuation step-up from prior round under a year ago
leads.source: BusinessWire / Bloomberg / Latitude Media — GridCARE Series A coverage
leads.notes: Combined 79, B/A borderline. $64M oversubscribed Series A from a marquee investor set (Sutter Hill, John Doerr, National Grid Partners) with explicit category-creation messaging ('Power Acceleration for AI'). Proof assets are unusually strong for Series A: validated PGE partnership unlocking 400MW in Hillsboro, $10B economic value claim. FIT lower than top FS-pure lead because energy/grid infrastructure is adjacent rather than core FS/tech, but B2B sales motion to utilities + data-center developers is crisp. Wedge: explicit category-narrative play — they ALREADY say they're creating a new category, so they need a PR partner who can land that story in WSJ/Bloomberg/Axios, plus thought leadership for the founder around AI power constraints. High urgency: AI-power story is the hottest narrative of 2026 and the window is open NOW.

Strong B2B sales motion (already $2B in healthcare AI initiatives tracked on the platform). Fit moderated by healthcare-AI vertical (adjacent to Mark's core FS/tech wheelhouse, but B2B-SaaS-to-enterprise pattern fits). Wedge: ROAI category-creation story + healthcare AI thought leadership for CIOs/CTOs. Lower FIT than pure-FS lead but a clean trigger; treat as a B-priority outreach if Mark has comfort with healthcare-AI accounts.

leads.notes: Combined 71, B. $17M add-on funding 5/13 led by Sound Ventures; $34M total raised. Deployed across 20+ health systems averaging $4B in annual net patient revenue — meaningful enterprise B2B traction. Fit pulled down by healthcare-payer-tech vertical (not core FS/tech), but enterprise sales motion and budget profile fit nicely. Mark-fit check needed on healthcare vertical depth before outreach.

leads.name: Meridian Ventures
leads.industry: Venture capital — pre-seed and seed-stage enterprise technology, focused on MBA-deferred founders
leads.fundingStage: Fund I — $35M launched 2026-05-15 by Devon Gethers and Karlton Haney; emerging-manager Fund I targeting US enterprise technology founders, particularly those deferring MBA programs
leads.source: TechCrunch — Meridian Ventures Fund I launch
leads.notes: Combined 65, B. Fund I emerging-manager launch 5/15 — by definition the target profile for fund-formation work, both ongoing (Fund I administration, SPVs, side letters, follow-on financings, eventual Fund II prep) AND new (any side vehicles around the core fund). FIT pulled down by 'fit' rubric weighting proof assets and B2B sales motion, which apply less cleanly to a VC firm than to an operating company. INTENT moderated because they've already announced close (legal counsel for formation is presumably already engaged); the realistic wedge is fractional/overflow legal counsel for ongoing fund maintenance, portfolio company support, and Fund II runway. Confirmed GPs: Devon Gethers and Karlton Haney.

leads.notes: Combined 70, B. Explicit fractional GC role at a Web3 infrastructure company with deep regulatory scope (blockchain + financial regulations). Strong INTENT score: posted as fractional, remote, easy apply via Built In. FIT capped because this is GC/governance work rather than fund formation or pure private-funds practice — but the regulatory and commercial-contracts work is squarely in Mark's wheelhouse, and the engagement model is explicit. The 'comp_stated' partial credit reflects market norms for Built In fractional postings (range typically disclosed in body even if not headline). Apply same-day or refer to Mark's bench.
